title: College/University Entrance Support - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Education > College/University Entrance Support 9 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Hispanic/Latino Community Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ANTFARM AXIS LEARNING CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a youth education center with academic and youth support services. Provides 13 computers to create, facilitate learning, and access to information. Assisting young people with completion of high school, establishment of educational goals, and movement towards college and/or work readiness are core services offered. Community members offer support through tutoring, mentoring, community volunteering, job-skills training, supported employment, and assisting young people with problem solving and critical thinking skills. 39140 Proctor Boulevard Sandy, OR 97055 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 668-9955 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHEHALEM YOUTH & FAMILY SERVICES YOOP! MCMINNVILLE SKILL CENTER YOUTH OPPORTUNITY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational and vocational opportunities for youth and young adults. Services include pre-employment training, Rethinking Careers Workshops, GED preparation classes, college application assistance, student financial aid assistance. Offers assistance in obtaining documentation such as ID card, Social Security card, and birth certificate. Parenting classes available for youth who have children. Child care is not available at skill centers, but vouchers may be available. 117 NE 5th Street Suite H McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Ages 16-24. Must meet income guidelines. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm by appointment Email (503) 883-9768 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHEHALEM YOUTH & FAMILY SERVICES YOOP! NEWBERG SKILL CENTER YOUTH OPPORTUNITY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational and vocational opportunities for youth and young adults. Services include pre-employment training, Rethinking Careers Workshops, GED preparation classes, college application assistance, student financial aid assistance. Offers assistance in obtaining documentation such as ID card, Social Security card, and birth certificate. Parenting classes available for youth who have children. Child care is not available at skill centers, but vouchers may be available. 100 E 5th Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Ages 16-24. Must meet income guidelines. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 554-1461 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IFOSTER FOSTER YOUTH RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Online resource for foster and former foster youth to access programs that help provide various services PO Box 159 Truckee, CA 96160 Eligibility: Current and former foster children ages 16-24 (older ages may be accepted). Current and former kinship youth, and crossover youth Hours: Not applicable Email (855) 936-7837 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO NETWORK ESCALERA Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Intensive college preparation program. Includes 80-hour internship, college visits, career exploration through job shadows, college planning lessons, help with financial aid and scholarship applications, high school credit support, STEM exploration, and leadership development. 410 NE 18th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Latino students in grades 11-12 Hours: Hours vary Email (503) 283-6881 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO NETWORK EARLY ESCALERA Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF College and career preparation program. College visits, career exploration, and planning lessons to help understand the financial aid and scholarship process, high school credit support, STEM exploration, and leadership development. 410 NE 18th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Latino students in grades 9-10 Hours: Hours vary Email (503) 283-6881 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT ALTERNATIVE PATHWAYS/TRIO Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Alternative Pathways is an educational program designed to assist alternative high school students in pursuing a postsecondary education. Services provided include weekly postsecondary education prep courses, one-on-one academic advising and transition planning, college tours, guest speakers, parent information workshops, and assistance with college applications, financial aid, and scholarship applications. Serves 11 alternative high schools and programs in Multnomah County. 11611 NE Ainsworth Circle Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Income restrictions, enrollment in Multnomah County alternative high school, desire to pursue postsecondary education Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 257-1654 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER COLLEGE AND CAREER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides career exploration, school search and exploration, resources for standardized test preparation, admissions and scholarship assistance, and educational workshops. Virtual service only. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: -Scholarships must be a current or former NAYA participants and pursuing a post-secondary degree -All other services:Grades 9-12 and who self-identify as Native American Hours: No standard hours Email (971) 325-4803 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH NEW AVENUES COLUMBIA STREET AVENUES TO COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps students, through one-on-one coaching, explore college options, apply, and get support after enrolling. Also provides financial assistance for housing and living expenses, budgeting guidance and scholarships, and information about other available resources. 1220 SW Columbia Street Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Must have 180 days of placement service after the age of 14 Ages 16-24 Must have a current or family-of-origin address within the City of Portland Hours: Application deadline is last day of May Email (503) 206-1666 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers GED preparation and information on how to access college. Offers one-on-one support from teachers and tutors. Participates currently enrolled in case management with New Avenues for Youth may be eligible for an educational scholarship who are attending career technical education or postsecondary education!. 314 SW 9th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 who are at-risk or experiencing housing instability Hours: Tuesday-Thursday 10am-1pm and 2pm-4pm Email (503) 358-9142 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ANTFARM AXIS LEARNING CENTER CHEHALEM YOUTH & FAMILY SERVICES YOUTH OPPORTUNITY PROGRAM CHEHALEM YOUTH & FAMILY SERVICES YOUTH OPPORTUNITY PROGRAM LATINO NETWORK ESCALERA MULTNOMAH EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT ALTERNATIVE PATHWAYS/TRIO NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER COLLEGE AND CAREER SERVICES NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH AVENUES TO COLLEGE NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH EDUCATION PROGRAM
